pr.title: Matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress by rewriting the loop and pulling allocations out of the loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: PR 2571 matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress in src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c by changing the inner readdressing loop from a count-mutating do-while guarded by if(count > 0) into an indexed for loop, moving count and j declarations to the function scope, and changing one pointer advance expression to uintptr_t-based addition. The bot report records one new match for main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth/HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, from 96.56% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress loop and pointer arithmetic rewrite.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress hunk at lines around 355-383

what_changed.text: Added function-scope locals int count and int j in HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added `int count;` and `int j;` near existing locals

what_changed.text: Changed `int count = *(int*) (p + 8);` inside the outer while loop to assignment into the predeclared `count` local.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replaced block-local count declaration with `count = *(int*) (p + 8);`

what_changed.text: Replaced `if (count > 0) { do { ... } while (--count != 0); }` with `for (j = 0; j < count; j++) { ... }`, preserving the same q-field updates and q += 0x40 stride inside the loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inner loop rewrite in HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress

what_changed.text: Changed pointer advancement from `p += (count << 6);` to `p = (u8*) ((count << 6) + (uintptr_t) p);` before the following `p += 0x10;`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pointer advance expression rewrite after inner loop

what_changed.text: The automated report recorded one new match: `main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth` item `HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress`, +9 bytes, 96.56% before and 100.00% after.

smart_moves.text: Kept the repeated q-offset updates readable inside a natural indexed loop rather than a count-mutating do-while shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replacement of guarded do-while with `for (j = 0; j < count; j++)`

smart_moves.text: Preserved the original `count` value for the post-loop `p` stride calculation by no longer decrementing `count` in the loop condition.
Used a uintptr_t cast in the `p` advancement expression, apparently to influence the matched arithmetic/codegen shape without changing the visible stride.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an inner loop decrements a count that is also needed after the loop for a stride calculation, try an indexed `for` loop with a separate counter so the original count remains available for later codegen.
scope: matching functions with post-loop stride arithmetic
evidence_refs: raw_diff: changed `do ... while (--count != 0)` to `for (j = 0; j < count; j++)`
raw_diff: post-loop `p` advancement still depends on `count << 6`

decomp_lessons.lesson: For pointer stride expressions in matching work, expression shape can matter: a cast through uintptr_t for address arithmetic may be worth testing when semantically equivalent pointer addition does not match.

naming_conventions.name: j
naming_conventions.convention: Short integer loop-counter names were used for the recovered indexed loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added `int j;` and used `for (j = 0; j < count; j++)`

naming_conventions.name: count
naming_conventions.convention: A short semantic local name `count` was retained for the value loaded from `p + 8` and used for both loop bound and stride calculation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Rewrite guarded do-while decrement loop into indexed for loop.
result_evidence: text_corpus bot report: 1 new match; HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress 96.56% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Move loop-bound and loop-index locals to function scope to alter local lifetime/declaration shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added function-scope `int count;` and `int j;`
result_evidence: text_corpus bot report: 1 new match; HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress 96.56% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use uintptr_t in pointer-add expression for p stride update.
result_evidence: text_corpus bot report: 1 new match; HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress 96.56% to 100.00%

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number, title, author, state, timestamps, changed file counts
raw_diff: complete 1406-byte diff for the single changed file
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report with one new match and before/after percentages
notes: PR body is empty and there are no review comments, so there is no human review rationale beyond the title and diff.
The PR title says 'pulling allocations out of the loop', but the diff evidence shows local declarations moved to function scope; this intake does not infer heap allocation behavior.
The bot report supports the claimed match for HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, but no local build command, objdiff output, or adjacent-function regression evidence is included in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve count values with indexed loops when post-loop stride arithmetic depends on count
accepted_candidate_records.text: In HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, replacing a decrementing `do ... while (--count != 0)` loop with `for (j = 0; j < count; j++)` preserved `count` for the subsequent `count << 6` pointer stride and coincided with a reported 100% match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: loop rewrite and post-loop count use

accepted_candidate_records.title: uintptr_t pointer-add expression shape may affect match
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR changed `p += (count << 6)` to `p = (u8*) ((count << 6) + (uintptr_t) p)` in a function that the bot report marked as newly matched. Treat this as a narrow tactic to test, not a general style rule.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: one new match

rejection_notes: Do not promote the title phrase 'pulling allocations out of the loop' as a verified allocation lesson; the diff only shows local declarations moved and loop structure changed.
Do not infer reviewer intent or review standards; the slice contains no review comments.
Do not promote a global rule that uintptr_t pointer arithmetic should be preferred generally; this PR only supports it as a narrow matching tactic in one function.
